materialChanges: Revised the transfer scene to distinguish an unweighted mean of three point sensors from a chamber-volume average and to require a representative, volume-aware sampling design before claiming corroboration or provisional average-only use.

Revised the transfer assessment stimulus, answer, reasoning, acceptable response, common-error feedback, and rubric so learners must test representativeness, reject unsupported point-to-volume aggregation, and preserve the separate evidence against a uniform zone value.

Updated ATTRIBUTION.md to describe the frozen-candidate reviews, the single post-review correction, and the location and limits of the final adjudication record without changing lesson identity, version, status, or source-confidence state.

reviewDispositions.rationale: The transfer scene and mastery item now state that the unweighted sensor mean estimates only the sampled-location mean, require equal-volume representation or defensible volume weights before treating it as a chamber-volume estimate, and withhold corroboration and provisional average-only use until representative independent evidence exists.

Independent recalculation in this finalization reproduced the worked and assessment pressures, proportional directions, units, sensitivity endpoints, and bounded ideal-gas interpretation; the affirmed pressure content was preserved.

The final audit confirmed that every mapped claim remains exposed in each relevant scene's source note with the complete supporting-source union and complete reference records. No ledger change was needed; the finding's stated sampling exception was resolved in the transfer scene and assessment.

The transfer explanation now explicitly separates the point-sensor mean from the chamber-volume average, identifies the missing representativeness and weighting basis, and teaches a volume-aware spatial test before any average corroboration claim.

The transfer item's prompt, stimulus, answer key, feedback, acceptable response, retry, and both affected rubric criteria now require representativeness checking and no longer award the invalid point-mean-to-volume-mean inference.
The prerequisite diagnostic continues to elicit a prediction under fixed conditions before feedback and retains a concrete, non-punitive recovery frame; no defect was identified.

The worked pressure audit still contains the task, represented givens, model selection, plan, reasoned execution, units and assumptions, independent check, interpretation, and self-explanation prompt affirmed by the reviewer.

The fit-means-true misconception sequence retains prediction, neutral statement of the wrong inference, a discriminating counterexample, a bounded replacement model, and a changed-case recheck; no defect was identified.

evidenceChecked: All six scenes retain one level-one heading, logical source order, descriptive section headings, prompts before feedback, and untimed text-based routes that do not require pointer gestures or hidden interaction state.

The ideal-gas equations are stated in words, every table can be read row by row, and the revised transfer scene repeats the sensor values, unweighted mean, spatial variation, and missing weighting basis in an equivalent prose route.

The declarative model-use diagram contains concise alt text and an outcome-equivalent long description of every node, decision branch, and return path; no learning conclusion depends only on layout or color.

Source inspection found no raw HTML, JavaScript, remote embed, Markdown image, unsafe TeX command, active asset, executable notebook, path traversal, sound, motion, hover-only disclosure, drag-only task, or time-only requirement in the pack.
All records and cases are explicitly fictional, require no physical experiment or sensory judgment, and contain no personal, patient, learner, credential, secret, or identifiable health data.
Every material claim remains mapped to the relevant scenes and learner-visible source notes, and each note lists the complete source union declared for those claims.

All three references are recorded as facts-only factual uses with original expression, concrete rights-basis evidence, dated checked-no-agent-restriction-found access records, exact terms locations, and no human-only source.

lesson.json declares no third-party assets; the only diagram is identified in ATTRIBUTION.md as original course content licensed with the CC BY 4.0 pack, and no quotation, adaptation, media redistribution, or dataset extraction is declared or present.

ATTRIBUTION.md identifies the accountable principal, contributor licence, authoring and packaging agents, run IDs, instruction digests, original-synthesis boundary, source-access preflight, excluded source, asset status, and the finalizer record in adjudication.json.
limitations: This finalization inspected semantic source and structured alternatives but did not run a live screen reader, browser keyboard session, 200% zoom/reflow render, reduced-motion mode, print render, or unavailable non-web export adapters.

This run did not reopen external sources or independently recheck current source terms; it audited the complete dated rights ledgers and relied on the academic review's declared direct source checks. No third-party expression, media, or dataset is reused.

Per the operator's explicit boundary, npm source preflight and repository validation were not run. JSON parsing, heading, prohibited-content, arithmetic, rights-state, and cross-file integrity checks passed locally, but they do not replace the repository gates.

The final content commit does not yet exist. Commit ancestry, final-content immutability, exact GitHub submission equivalence, and runtime-banner provenance stamping remain operator checks rather than claims verified by this run.

rationale: The original candidate and both complete advisory reviews were read against the same candidate commit. The supplied COMMENTED transport state is eligible for the PR author's standard-lesson reviews and was not treated as dismissal. The records supply exactly one academic and one learning-design input, two request-changes verdicts, two distinct declared agent runs, and two distinct declared model families across OpenAI and Google; these provenance values are disclosed and attested, not independently verified. Both reviewers identified the same material sampling-and-aggregation error. The single final revision corrects that error consistently in instruction, nonvisual equivalence, assessment logic, feedback, retry guidance, and scoring while preserving the academically and pedagogically sound material identified in the remaining findings. Recalculation and local structural checks passed, claims and sources remain aligned, and the final accessibility-and-rights audit found no unresolved academic, access, privacy, security, attribution, or reuse blocker. A merge decision is therefore warranted subject to the operator creating and recording the final content commit, stamping runtime provenance, and passing the required repository gates without further teaching-content changes.
dissent: Both eligible reviewers issued request-changes verdicts on the original candidate because it conflated a matching unweighted point-sensor mean with corroboration of a chamber-volume average. That dissent is accepted and incorporated; neither reviewer is represented as having reviewed or approved the final edit.
conditions: The operator must commit the final lesson content with both cited review artifacts while excluding adjudication.json from that content commit, then replace PENDING-FINAL-COMMIT with the resulting exact 40-character SHA.
The operator must replace each RUNTIME-STAMPED adjudicator system, provider, model, and version value from the runtime banner without changing the run ID or instructions digest.

Strict source preflight and the repository's required sequential verification or validation gates must pass after stamping; any failure that requires teaching-content changes makes this adjudication stale.

After the recorded final content commit, only the separately authorized publication transition for lesson status, source confidence, and retained claim review-status fields may occur without new adjudication.

adjudicator.displayName: VSBDev — fresh finalizer and adjudication operator
